metadata.title: Guideline on the Clinical Requirements for Non-Replacement Therapy in Haemophilia A and B

rules.rule_text: A thorough dose-PK/PD characterisation is required before phase III. Due to the mechanisms of action of non-replacement therapies, thrombogenic risk cannot be excluded, making comprehensive dose-finding of particular importance.
rules.verbatim_quote: Due to the mechanisms of action of non-replacement therapies, a thrombogenic risk cannot be excluded. Therefore, comprehensive dose-finding is of particular importance before initiating phase III trials.

rules.rule_text: Patients with and without inhibitors must not be included in one study arm or have data pooled, as these patients are not comparable concerning baseline characteristics and standard of care.
rules.verbatim_quote: it is not considered meaningful to include patients with and without inhibitors into one study arm or to pool data from patients with and without inhibitors as these patients are not comparable concerning baseline characteristics and standard of care.

rules.rule_text: When both HA and HB patients are included in one study, a sufficient number of patients for each disease must be enrolled to allow meaningful subgroup analyses. Separate analyses must always be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: However, a sufficient number of patients for each disease needs to be enrolled in order to allow meaningful subgroup analyses. ... even when combined analyses may be acceptable, separate analyses should also always be provided, as mentioned above in section 4.2.1.
rules.exceptions: Combined analysis is only acceptable when scientifically justified based on mode of action.

rules.rule_text: Stratification according to disease severity is required. The intended posology must be well justified for each disease severity level, with particular attention to avoiding overdosing in moderate haemophilia patients with higher endogenous FVIII/IX levels to prevent thrombosis risk.
rules.verbatim_quote: In any case, stratification according to severity is required. Furthermore, the intended posology needs to be well justified for each disease severity. It is important to avoid overdosing in haemophilia patients in general but also specifically in haemophilia patients with moderate haemophilia and higher endogenous factor VIII/IX levels to prevent a potential increased risk of thrombosis.

rules.rule_text: The primary endpoint must be annualised bleeding rate (ABR) of all bleeds (both spontaneous and traumatic). Bleeding definitions must follow scientifically established definitions and be consistent between run-in and active treatment phases.
rules.verbatim_quote: The variable for the primary endpoint should be ABR of all bleeds, i.e. both spontaneous and traumatic bleeds. ... Definition of bleeds e.g. severity, should be laid down in the study protocol and should follow scientifically established definitions. ... Importantly, the same definitions should be used during the run-in phase as well as the active treatment phase.
rules.exceptions: Bleeds due to surgery/procedure should not be included in the primary analysis but should be captured by the study protocol.

rules.rule_text: The primary efficacy assessment must be based on intra-patient comparisons between the observational (run-in) phase and the treatment phase of the study.
rules.verbatim_quote: The primary efficacy assessment should be based on intra-patient comparisons between the observational (run-in) and the treatment phase of the study.

rules.rule_text: For non-inhibitor patients, the primary endpoint must assess non-inferiority of ABR of prophylaxis with the new non-replacement product vs pre-study prophylactic treatment. Superiority over on-demand treatment alone is not sufficient.
rules.verbatim_quote: The primary endpoint for the treatment of non-inhibitor patients with non-factor replacement therapies will assess non-inferiority of prophylaxis in terms of ABR with the investigational medicinal product versus pre-study prophylactic treatment. ... demonstration of superiority of a new non-replacement therapy used prophylactically over on-demand treatment is not considered sufficient.

rules.rule_text: For inhibitor patients, the primary endpoint must assess either superiority of prophylaxis relative to pre-study on-demand treatment, or non-inferiority vs pre-study prophylactic treatment, depending on the therapy patients received during the run-in period.
rules.verbatim_quote: in inhibitor patients, the primary endpoint will assess either superiority of prophylaxis relative to pre-study on-demand treatment or non-inferiority of prophylaxis versus pre-study prophylactic treatment depending on the therapy that inhibitor patients received during the run-in period of the study.

rules.rule_text: Subgroup analysis to assess consistency of treatment effect must be provided for relevant subgroups including HA, HB, +/- inhibitors, and age.
rules.verbatim_quote: Subgroup analysis to assess consistency of the treatment effect should be provided for relevant subgroups (e.g., HA, HB, +/- inhibitors, age).

rules.rule_text: A run-in period of at least 6 months is required for prospective collection of baseline data to enable intra-patient comparison. A washout period between run-in and active treatment should be considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: a lead-in period of at least 6 months is considered acceptable for intra-patient comparison. ... A washout period between run-in phase and active treatment phase to avoid a carryover effect of prior treatment should be considered.
rules.exceptions: If a sufficiently long washout period is not feasible due to increased risk of bleeding, the start timepoint of evaluating efficacy needs to be justified.

rules.rule_text: The overall treatment phase with the investigational product must be at least 12 months to allow reliable conclusions on efficacy and identify safety risks.
rules.verbatim_quote: the overall treatment phase with the investigational medicinal product is recommended to be at least 12 months to allow reliable conclusions on the efficacy and identify safety risks associated with these new medicinal products.

rules.rule_text: Evaluation of treatment effect by comparison against historical data not prospectively collected is not recommended for the primary analysis, though it may serve as supportive evidence.
rules.verbatim_quote: Evaluation of the treatment effect by comparison against historical data, i.e. external data that are not prospectively collected according to the same definitions as laid down in the study protocol for the active treatment phase and in the same patients, is not recommended for the primary analysis but may serve as supportive evidence for the benefit-risk assessment.

rules.rule_text: Secondary endpoints must include factor/bypassing agents consumption, number of target joints, improvement in target joints, annualised joint/traumatic/spontaneous bleeding rate, percentage of patients with no bleeds, and health-related quality of life.
rules.verbatim_quote: Supportive data should be collected through secondary endpoints such as factor/bypassing agents' consumption, number of target joints, improvement in target joints, annualised joint/traumatic/spontaneous bleeding rate, percentage of patients with no bleeds and health-related quality of life.

rules.rule_text: Safety-related secondary endpoints must specifically capture thrombotic events (incidence and severity), immunogenicity, and infusion/injection site reactions.
rules.verbatim_quote: Safety-related (secondary) endpoints should specifically capture the incidence and severity of thrombotic events, immunogenicity and infusion/injection site reactions.

rules.rule_text: The estimand of primary interest must be carefully considered with handling of intercurrent events defined in the study protocol, applicable to both run-in and active treatment periods.
rules.verbatim_quote: The estimand of primary interest needs to be carefully considered, taking into account the specific setting of the disease and the treatment. The handling of intercurrent events needs to be defined in the study protocol, together with a definition of the primary (and secondary) estimand(s). This applies to both the run-in and the active treatment period.

rules.rule_text: The active treatment phase must be at least 6 months at steady PD state to characterise efficacy and identify safety risks. An adequate number of each haemophilia subtype (HA, HB, +/- inhibitors) must be included in the safety database.
rules.verbatim_quote: the active treatment phase should be at least 6 months (at PD state) to characterise the long-term safety and detect potential safety risks (e.g. severe bleedings, thrombotic complications) and increase the likelihood of detecting unexpected complications associated with these therapies.

rules.rule_text: Thrombotic events must be defined as Adverse Events of Special Interest (AESI). Thrombotic microangiopathy and DIC must be specifically named to avoid overlooking clinical manifestations. Thromboembolic risk must be separately evaluated for HA and HB patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: Thrombotic events should be defined as Adverse Event of special Interest (AESI). Depending on the product's specific mode of action, thrombotic microangiopathy and disseminated intravascular coagulation should be specifically named in order to avoid overlooking clinical manifestations of these AEs. Additionally, the risk of thromboembolic complications should be separately evaluated for HA and HB patients as there might be differences due to concomitant medications.

rules.rule_text: Immunogenicity for anti-drug-antibodies (ADA) and neutralising antibodies (nAB) must be tested in accordance with respective guidelines.
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunogenicity for anti-drug-antibodies (ADA) and nAB should be tested in accordance with the respective guidelines.

rules.rule_text: Paediatric clinical development must follow a stepwise approach: adults first, then adolescents (>=12 years), then children <12 years only after sufficient adult/adolescent experience. A dedicated paediatric study is required for children <12 years.
rules.verbatim_quote: the clinical development in the paediatric population should follow a stepwise approach to ensure that there is some experience in adults before clinical investigation is started in children. The initial age cohort of haemophilia A and B paediatric patients to be investigated is ≥12 years of age (adolescents). ... The clinical trial(s) in children <12 years of age should not start before sufficient experience with the new non-factor replacement therapy has been gained in adults and patients ≥12 years of age. The efficacy and safety profile of novel non-factor replacement therapies in patients <12 years of age should be investigated in a dedicated paediatric study.
rules.exceptions: Inclusion of children >=12 years together with adults in phase III may be acceptable depending on available data.

rules.rule_text: Paediatric study must include an adequate number of children aged 6 to <12 years and <6 years to allow meaningful benefit-risk assessment in all age groups. Clinical investigation in children must be agreed by an approved PIP.
rules.verbatim_quote: An adequate number of children aged 6 to <12 years of age and <6 years of age should be included to allow for a meaningful benefit-risk assessment in all age groups. ... The clinical investigation in children needs to be agreed by an approved PIP.
rules.exceptions: In certain cases, extrapolation may be acceptable for some age groups but must be well justified.

rules.rule_text: Data to support recommendations for managing bleeding events and surgeries with additional coagulation or bypassing agents must be provided for the paediatric population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Data to support recommendations on how to clinically manage bleeding events and surgeries in terms of additional coagulation or bypassing agents will also be required for the paediatric population where traumas through falls and acute surgeries (e.g. appendix, teeth, adenoids) are common.

rules.rule_text: Post-authorisation data collection through registries and/or PASS/PAES is required due to limited pre-approval safety data. Core data elements from the 2018 EMA haemophilia registries workshop must be collected.
rules.verbatim_quote: Due to the rare nature of the disease and patient availability for clinical studies, safety data will be limited pre-approval. Therefore, additional data may need to be collected post-marketing through registries and/or a dedicated Post-Authorisation Safety Study (PASS)/Post-Authorisation Efficacy Study (PAES). The core data elements required to be collected in registries can be found in the report of the agreed outcome of the haemophilia registries workshop from 2018
